| 3rdRegional cup first = [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup|2003]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = '''Champions''' ([[2013 SAFF Championship|2013]])

{{Medal|Competition|[[SAFF Championship]]}}
{{MedalGold|[[2013 SAFF Championship|2013 Nepal]]|Team}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2011 SAFF Championship|2011 India]]|Team}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2015 SAFF Championship|2015 India]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Afghanistan national football team''' represents [[Afghanistan]] in men's international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Afghanistan Football Federation]]. The [[Afghanistan Football Federation]] was established in 1922 and joined [[FIFA]] in 1948. It was also one of the founding members of the [[Asian Football Confederation]] (AFC) in 1954.<ref>{{Cite web |title=History of Afghanistan national football team |url=http://www.afghan-web.com/sports/sports_history.html |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160202235038/http://www.afghan-web.com/sports/sports_history.html |archive-date=2 February 2016 |website=Afghan-web.com}}</ref>

Afghanistan claimed their first international football title and marked a historic moment for Afghan football when they became champions of the [[2013 SAFF Championship|2013]] tournament of the [[SAFF Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|title=SAFF Championship 2013|url=http://www.saffchampionship.org/matches.php|publisher=SAFF Championship|accessdate=5 October 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150107231614/http://www.saffchampionship.org/matches.php|archive-date=7 January 2015|url-status=dead|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

== History ==
=== Early history ===
{{multiple image
| align             = left
| direction         = vertical
| image1            = Afghanistan at the 1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament.jpg
| width1            = 
| image2            = Afghanistan national football team at the 1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament.jpg
| footer            = Afghanistan at the [[1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament]]
| total_width       = 
| width2            = 
}}

Afghanistan played its first official international match under FIFA at the [[1948 Summer Olympics]], where it faced [[Luxembourg national football team|Luxembourg]] on 26 July and lost 6–0. 

Before 2002, the last recorded international appearance for the team was in the [[1984 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], where they lost 6–1 to [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]] on 20 September. The team did not participate in international football between 1984 and 2002 due to political instability, including the [[Soviet–Afghan War|Soviet invasion]], [[Afghan Civil War (1992–1996)|civil war]], and [[Taliban]] rule, which banned recreational activities such as football.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Afghan Football Veterans, Experts Reflect on Sports' Growth |url=https://tolonews.com/afghanistan/afghan-football-veterans-experts-reflect-sports-growth |date=2013-09-12 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220912092338/https://tolonews.com/afghanistan/afghan-football-veterans-experts-reflect-sports-growth |archive-date=2022-09-12 |access-date=2022-09-12 |website=Tolo News}}</ref>

=== 2002–2009 ===
[[File:Afg-tkm-2003.jpg|thumb|Match scene between Afghanistan and Turkmenistan in a [[FIFA World Cup]] qualification match in [[Kabul]], 2003|left]]
Following the fall of the Taliban regime, Afghanistan re-entered the international football scene in 2002.

In 2003, the national team competed in the [[2003 SAFF Gold Cup]], but suffered defeats in all group stage matches against [[India national football team|India]], [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan]], and [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]].

Later that year, Afghanistan took part in the [[2004 AFC Asian Cup qualification]], managing a win against [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]] but falling short after a loss to [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]], thus failing to advance. The team also entered the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|2006 FIFA World Cup qualification]] for the first time, where they were eliminated by [[Turkmenistan national football team|Turkmenistan]] with a 13–0 aggregate score.

In the [[2005 SAFF Gold Cup]] held in [[Karachi]], Pakistan, Afghanistan was defeated by the [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffgold05.html|title=South Asian Gold Cup 2005 (Karachi, Pakistan)|access-date=29 December 2023|archive-date=24 December 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171224024944/http://rsssf.com/tabless/saffgold05.html|url-status=live}}</ref> During the inaugural [[2006 AFC Challenge Cup]], the team earned draws against both [[Chinese Taipei national football team|Chinese Taipei]] and the [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]], with goals from [[Hafizullah Qadami]] and [[Sayed Maqsood Hashemi]] securing the results.

In the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification]], Afghanistan was knocked out by [[Syria national football team|Syria]]. The team participated in the [[2008 SAFF Championship]], where they lost to [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] and drew with [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] and [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]]. However, they managed to top their qualification group for the [[2008 AFC Challenge Cup]] but were eliminated in the final tournament after three consecutive defeats to [[India national football team|India]], [[Tajikistan national football team|Tajikistan]], and [[Turkmenistan national football team|Turkmenistan]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/en/challenge-cup-previous-competitions/afc-challenge-cup-2008/|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20130213160640/http://www.the-afc.com/en/challenge-cup-previous-competitions/afc-challenge-cup-2008/|url-status=dead|title=Afghanistan at AFC Challenge Cup 2008 at AFC Website|archivedate=13 February 2013}}</ref>

In the [[2009 SAFF Championship]], Afghanistan lost all three group matches—against [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]], [[India national football team|India]], and [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]]—with their sole goal of the tournament scored by [[Hashmatullah Barakzai]] in a 3–1 loss to the [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffgold09.html|title=South Asian Gold Cup 2009 (Dhaka)|access-date=29 December 2023|archive-date=9 December 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221209173528/https://www.rsssf.org/tabless/saffgold09.html|url-status=live}}</ref>

=== 2010–2019 ===
Afghanistan entered their third [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (AFC)|World Cup qualification campaign]] and faced [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] in the preliminary round. The first leg was played in Tajikistan due to security concerns and ended in defeat. The second leg in Palestine ended in a 1–1 draw, with [[Balal Arezou]] scoring for Afghanistan, resulting in their elimination.

==== 2011 SAFF Championship runners-up ====
[[File:Ahmad Faisal - football - B.jpg|thumb|left|Players are celebrating after winning their [[2011 SAFF Championship]] Semi-final against Nepal]]

In the [[2011 SAFF Championship]], Afghanistan achieved a dominant 8–1 victory over [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]], marking their second-biggest win in history. [[Balal Arezou]] scored four goals in that match. Afghanistan advanced to the semi-finals for the first time, defeating [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] 1–0 after extra time, with Arezou scoring in the 101st minute. In the final, they lost 4–0 to [[India national football team|India]].

Afghanistan progressed from the first round of the [[2012 AFC Challenge Cup qualification]] by defeating [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] over two legs. In the second round, they were eliminated after losses to [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] and [[North Korea national football team|North Korea]], despite a win over [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]].

==== 2013 SAFF Championship winners ====
{{See also|2013 SAFF Championship Final}}
{{football squad on pitch|align=left
|  GK = [[Mansur Faqiryar|'''Faqiryar''']]
|  RB = [[Mustafa Hadid|'''Hadid''']]
| RCB = '''Ataie'''
| LCB = [[Zohib Islam Amiri|'''Amiri (C)''']]
|  LB = [[Mujtaba Faiz|'''Faiz''']]
|  RM = [[Mohammad Mashriqi|'''Mashriqi''']]
|  CM = [[Mohammad Rafi Barekzay|'''Barekzay''']]
|  LM = [[Mohammad Marouf Jamhour|'''Marouf''']]
|  RF = [[Mustafa Azadzoy|'''Azadzoy''']]
|  CF = [[Sandjar Ahmadi|'''Ahmadi''']]
|  LF = [[Balal Arezou|'''Arezou''']] 
| caption = [[2013 SAFF Championship#Final|2013 SAFF Championship Final]] starting lineup on 11 September 2013, in [[Dasarath Stadium]] ([[Kathmandu]], [[Nepal]]).
}}
[[File:Hamid Karimi.jpg|thumb|right|[[Hamidullah Karimi]] with the [[2013 SAFF Championship]] trophy after their win against [[India men's national football team|India]]]]

Afghanistan entered the [[2013 SAFF Championship]] as the highest FIFA-ranked team in the tournament, standing at 139th globally. They opened the competition with a dominant 3–0 victory over [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]], with goals scored by [[Zohib Islam Amiri|Amiri]]—regarded as one of Afghanistan's greatest players—[[Mustafa Azadzoy|Azadzoy]], and [[Hashmatullah Barakzai|Barakzai]].

In their second group stage match, they continued their strong form with a 3–1 win over [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]], thanks to goals from [[Mohammad Rafi Barekzay|Rafi]], [[Zohib Islam Amiri|Amiri]], and [[Hashmatullah Barakzai|Barakzai]].

In the semi-finals, Afghanistan faced host nation [[Nepal national football team|Nepal]] and secured a hard-fought 1–0 win, with [[Sandjar Ahmadi]] scoring the only goal in the 11th minute.

The final was a rematch of the 2011 edition against [[India national football team|India]].<ref name="Faqiryar: A real football fairytale">{{cite news
| title = Faqiryar: A real football fairytale
| url = https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2014/m=5/news=faqiryar-ein-fussball-marchen-a-la-hollywood-2334499-2334671.html
| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20170315001050/https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2014/m=5/news=faqiryar-ein-fussball-marchen-a-la-hollywood-2334499-2334671.html
| url-status = dead
| archive-date = 15 March 2017
| publisher = fifa.com
| language= en
| date = 6 May 2014
| access-date = 14 March 2017}}</ref> This time, Afghanistan prevailed with a 2–0 victory, claiming their first-ever international football title and marking a historic moment for Afghan football.<ref name="Ahmadi reaping rewards for bravery">{{cite news
| title = Ahmadi reaping rewards for bravery
| url = https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2013/m=10/news=ahmadi-reaping-rewards-for-bravery-2202233.html
| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20170315001312/https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2013/m=10/news=ahmadi-reaping-rewards-for-bravery-2202233.html
| url-status = dead
| archive-date = 15 March 2017
| publisher = fifa.com
| language= en
| date = 22 October 2013
| access-date = 14 March 2017}}</ref>
[[File:Rafi.jpg|left|thumb|Afghanistan against [[Tajikistan national football team|Tajikistan]] on 4 May 2014]]


Afghanistan reached the semi-finals of the [[2014 AFC Challenge Cup]] but lost 2–0 to eventual champions Palestine. In the third-place match, they drew 1–1 with the Maldives and lost 8–7 in a penalty shootout. On 10 June 2014, the [[Afghanistan Football Federation]] moved from the [[South Asian Football Federation]] (SAFF) to the [[Central Asian Football Association]] (CAFA). Later that year, the federation received the [[FIFA Fair Play Award]] at the 2014 Ballon d'Or Gala for its efforts to promote football under difficult circumstances.[[File:Afghan national football team, 2018 FIFA World Cup qualification, Azadi Stadium.jpg|thumb|right|Afghan national team before the [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match against [[Japan national football team|Japan]], [[Azadi Stadium]]]]
On 9 February 2015, [[Slaven Skeledžić]] was appointed as head coach. The team started the qualifiers with a 6–0 loss to [[Syria national football team|Syria]] but earned its first-ever World Cup qualification victory against [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]]. After a poor run of results, Skeledžić resigned in October 2015. Peter Segrt took over in November and led the team to another win over Cambodia, 3–0.<ref name="Skeledzic: Afghan job my greatest challenge">{{cite news
| title = Skeledzic: Afghan job my greatest challenge
| url = https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2015/m=2/news=skeledzic-afghan-job-my-greatest-challenge-2532101.html
| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20170315000749/https://www.fifa.com/live-scores/news/y=2015/m=2/news=skeledzic-afghan-job-my-greatest-challenge-2532101.html
| url-status = dead
| archive-date = 15 March 2017
| publisher = fifa.com
| language= en
| date = 25 February 2015
| access-date = 14 March 2017}}</ref> On 27 April 2015 the AFF signed a contract with a new sponsor called Alokozay Group of Companies.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://tolonews.com/sports/aff-signs-mou-alakozai-group-companies|title=AFF Signs MoU with Alakozai Group of Companies|access-date=29 December 2023|archive-date=29 December 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231229161148/https://tolonews.com/sports/aff-signs-mou-alakozai-group-companies|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://sports.ndtv.com/football/india-to-host-2015-saff-cup-1530475|title=India to host 2015 SAFF Cup|access-date=29 December 2023|archive-date=29 December 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20231229161148/https://sports.ndtv.com/football/india-to-host-2015-saff-cup-1530475|url-status=live}}</ref>

==== 2015 SAFF Championship runners-up ====
{{See also|2015 SAFF Championship Final}}
[[File:Kani Taher.jpeg|thumb|Afghanistan against [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] at the [[2015 SAFF Championship]]]]

This tournament marked Afghanistan's final appearance in the SAFF Championship before moving to CAFA. The team won all its group stage matches—beating [[Bangladesh national football team|Bangladesh]] 4–0, [[Bhutan national football team|Bhutan]] 3–0, and [[Maldives national football team|Maldives]] 4–1. In the semi-final, they defeated [[Sri Lanka national football team|Sri Lanka]] 5–0. The final was a rematch with [[India men's national football team|India]], which Afghanistan lost 2–1 after extra time.

Following the SAFF Championship, Afghanistan had a chance to qualify for the [[2019 AFC Asian Cup]] through expanded qualifiers. After a second heavy loss to [[Japan national football team|Japan]] (5–0), they needed a win over [[Singapore national football team|Singapore]], which they secured 2–1. The team then played friendlies against [[Lebanon national football team|Lebanon]] (lost 2–0) and [[Malaysia national football team|Malaysia]] (1–1 draw). In February 2017, [[Otto Pfister]] was appointed as head coach. Afghanistan drew 1–1 with [[Vietnam national football team|Vietnam]] in the qualifiers, but losses to [[Cambodia national football team|Cambodia]] and [[Jordan national football team|Jordan]] ended their qualification hopes. In July 2018, the federation announced a friendly match against [[Palestine national football team|Palestine]] in Kabul—their first home game since 2013.<ref name="Otto Pfister, New Mentor For Afghanistan National Football Team">{{cite news
| title = Otto Pfister, New Mentor For Afghanistan National Football Team
| url = http://middleeastpress.com/english/otto-pfister-new-mentor-for-afghanistan-national-football-team/
| language = en
| date = 12 February 2017
| access-date = 12 February 2017
| archive-date = 13 July 2017
| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20170713164944/http://middleeastpress.com/english/otto-pfister-new-mentor-for-afghanistan-national-football-team/
| url-status = dead
}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://tnp.straitstimes.com/sports/singapore-football/lions-draw-positives-defeat|title=Lions draw positives from defeat|date=24 March 2017|publisher=TNP|access-date=17 February 2023|archive-date=10 November 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221110223709/https://tnp.straitstimes.com/sports/singapore-football/lions-draw-positives-defeat|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/afcasfeeds?fixtureid=11161&stageid=432&tMode=H&view=ajax&show=matchsummary|title=AFC Asian Cup match: AFG-VIE|date=28 March 2017|publisher=AFC|access-date=25 January 2017|archive-date=30 August 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170830193324/http://www.the-afc.com/afcasfeeds?fixtureid=11161&stageid=432&tMode=H&view=ajax&show=matchsummary|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.the-afc.com/news/afcsection/afghanistan-edge-cambodia|title=Afghanistan edge Cambodia|date=27 March 2018|publisher=AFC|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-date=3 August 2018|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180803163613/http://www.the-afc.com/news/afcsection/afghanistan-edge-cambodia|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|last1=Team |first1=Momtaz Dev |url=http://aff.org.af/afghanistan-football-federation-introduces-new-head-coach/|title=Afghanistan Football Federation introduces new head coach.|work=Afghanistan Football Federation |date=10 July 2018|publisher=AFF|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-date=15 April 2020|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200415144727/http://aff.org.af/afghanistan-football-federation-introduces-new-head-coach/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.gelderlander.nl/voetbal-nijmegen/nec-trainer-dastgir-nieuwe-bondscoach-van-afghanistan~ac2afbd3/|title=NEC-trainer Dastgir nieuwe bondscoach van Afghanistan|language=nl|website=Gelderlander|access-date=3 August 2018|archive-date=5 September 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210905054044/https://www.gelderlander.nl/voetbal-nijmegen/nec-trainer-dastgir-nieuwe-bondscoach-van-afghanistan~ac2afbd3/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.tolonews.com/sport/kabul%C2%A0-host-afghanistan-palestine%C2%A0friendly%C2%A0football-match|title=Kabul To Host Afghanistan-Palestine Friendly Football Match|website=ToloNews}}</ref>

=== 2020–present ===
{{multiple image
| perrow            = 2
| total_width       = 300
| align             = right
| image1            = Sebastian Rasmussen Running to the Ball Surrounded by Afghan Players - PHI vs AFG, September 12, 2023.png
| image2            = Faisal Hamidi Punches Ball Away - PHI vs AFG, September 12, 2023.png
| image3            = Neil Etheridge's High Ball Deflection and Save - PHI vs AFG, September 12, 2023.png
| image4            = 
| image5            = Jesse Curran's Header Attempt - PHI vs AFG, September 12, 2023.png
| footer            = Afghanistan against [[Philippines national football team|Philippines]] on 12 September 2023
}}

The [[2023 CAFA Nations Cup]] was the first edition of the [[CAFA Nations Cup]], the biennial international men's [[association football|football]] championship of [[Central Asia]] organized by the [[Central Asian Football Association]] (CAFA). The event was held in [[Kyrgyzstan]] and [[Uzbekistan]] from 10 to 20 June.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.tasnimnews.com/en/news/2023/03/14/2867675/iran-likely-to-meet-russia-in-2023-cafa-championship |title=Iran Likely to Meet Russia in 2023 CAFA Championship |work=Tasnim News Agency |access-date=2023-03-19 |archive-date=2023-03-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230319163504/https://www.tasnimnews.com/en/news/2023/03/14/2867675/iran-likely-to-meet-russia-in-2023-cafa-championship |url-status=live }}</ref> Afghanistan lost both games in Group B to [[Iran national football team|Iran]] and [[Kyrgyzstan national football team|Kyrgyzstan]], thus finishing in last place in the tournament.

The [[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]] was the second edition of the CAFA Nations Cup. The event was held in [[Tajikistan]] and [[Uzbekistan]] from 29 August to 8 September. Afghanistan was allocated in Group B and lost their first match against [[Iran national football team|Iran]]. Their second match was against [[Tajikistan national football team|Tajikistan]] on 1 September, which ended in a 0–2 defeat. Afghanistan's third and final match in Group B was against [[India national football team|India]] on 4 September, which ended in a 0–0 draw.

== Team image ==
=== Kit suppliers ===
{| class="wikitable"
! Kit manufacturer
! Period
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Adidas]]
| 2005–2008
|-
| {{flagicon|Denmark}} [[Hummel International|Hummel]]
| 2009–2020
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[Jako]]
| 2020–2023
|-
| {{flagicon|India}} SIX5SIX
| 2024–present
|}

=== Supporters ===
[[File:Afghanistan national football team supporter in Tehran.jpg|thumb|left|Afghan supporters watching their national team play against Japan during [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification]] in [[Azadi Stadium]], [[Tehran]].]]Afghanistan's national football team has attracted significant public support during major fixtures. In August 2013, the team's friendly against [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan]] drew a sell-out crowd for the first international match held in Kabul in a decade, with reports describing the occasion as a rare display of national unity across the country's ethnic and political divisions.<ref name="RFERL" /><ref name=":0" /> Later that year, Afghanistan's victory over [[India national football team|India]] in the [[2013 SAFF Championship]] final prompted widespread celebrations in Kabul and other parts of the country.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Afghanistan beat India |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2013/9/12/afghanistan-triumph-over-india |access-date=2026-06-26 |website=Al Jazeera |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=افغانستان |first=همکارانِ شبکه تحلیل گران |date=2013-09-12 |title=Football Victory II: A night and a day of celebrations |url=https://www.afghanistan-analysts.org/en/reports/context-culture/football-victory-ii-a-night-and-a-day-of-celebrations/ |access-date=2026-06-26 |website=Afghanistan Analysts Network - English |language=ps-GB}}</ref>

=== Rivalries ===
{{Further|Afghanistan–Pakistan football rivalry}}
{{multiple image
| align             = right
| direction         = horizontal
| image1            = Pakistan vs Afghanistan, 1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Football Tournament.jpg
| width1            = 
| image2            = Pakistan and Afghanistan captains during the 1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Football Tournament.jpg
| footer            = Pakistan vs Afghanistan at the [[1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament]]
| total_width       = 
| width2            = 
}}Afghanistan shares a [[Afghanistan–Pakistan sports rivalries#Football|sporting rivalry]] with [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan]], which extends into football alongside other sports.

The national teams of Pakistan and Afghanistan first met at the 1976 [[Afghanistan Republic Day Festival Cup]], where the A team of Afghanistan won by 1–0,<ref name=":05">{{Cite web |title=Afghanistan Republic Day Festival Cup (Kabul, Afghanistan) |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesj/jasson76.html#1976 |access-date=2024-07-15 |website=www.rsssf.org}}</ref><ref name=":0">{{Cite news |last=Graham-Harrison |first=Emma |date=2013-08-20 |title=Football match allows Afghanistan to forget its woes for 90 minutes |url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2013/aug/20/afghanistan-pakistan-football-match |access-date=2025-04-05 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=2013-08-20 |title=In First Meeting Since 1970s, Afghanistan Tops Pakistan In Soccer |url=https://www.wlrn.org/2013-08-20/in-first-meeting-since-1970s-afghanistan-tops-pakistan-in-soccer |access-date=2025-04-05 |website=WLRN |language=en}}</ref> with [[Mohammad Saber Rohparwar]] scoring the lone goal on a corner kick minutes before game's end.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Hodge |first=Nathan |date=2013-08-20 |title=A Pitch Battle Unites Afghans, Pakistanis |url=https://www.wsj.com/articles/a-pitch-battle-unites-afghans-pakistanis-1377027769 |access-date=2025-03-03 |work=Wall Street Journal |language=en-US |issn=0099-9660}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Afghanistan and Pakistan Braced to Face-Off in Kabul for First Time in 37 Years |url=https://tolonews.com/sports/afghanistan-and-pakistan-braced-face-kabul-first-time-37-years |access-date=2025-04-05 |website=TOLOnews |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Afghan Footballers Return From Abroad Ahead of Pakistan Match |url=https://tolonews.com/sports/afghan-footballers-return-abroad-ahead-pakistan-match |access-date=2025-04-05 |website=TOLOnews |language=en}}</ref> Because of rising tensions between the two countries in the 1970s, Afghan president [[Mohammad Daoud Khan]] handed each player 5,000 afghanis as a celebration and gift for their victory.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Pakistan National Team's Return to Kabul Echoes 1976 Jashen Celebration |url=https://tolonews.com/opinion/pakistan-national-teams-return-kabul-echoes-1976-jashen-celebration |access-date=2025-04-05 |website=TOLOnews |language=en}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Latifi |first=Ali M. |title=Politics kicked aside in Afghan football game |url=https://www.aljazeera.com/features/2013/8/22/politics-kicked-aside-in-afghan-football-game |access-date=2024-07-15 |website=Al Jazeera |language=en}}</ref> Both teams met again at the [[1976 Quaid-e-Azam International Tournament]] the same year where Pakistan avenged by winning by the same score, with forward [[Afzal Qasim]] scoring the winning goal in the eighth minute after the kick off.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Quaid-E-Azam International Cup (Pakistan) |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesq/quaid-e-azam.html#76 |access-date=2024-07-15 |website=www.rsssf.org}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Pakistan's National Team Arrives in Kabul |url=https://tolonews.com/sports/pakistans-national-team-arrives-kabul |access-date=2025-04-05 |website=TOLOnews |language=en}}</ref> 

The ensuing civil war and political instability in Afghanistan prevented the two sides from meeting again until 2003.<ref name="RFERL2222">{{cite news |last=Bezhan |first=Frud |date=21 August 2013 |title=Historic Soccer Match Brings Unity To Afghanistan |url=https://www.rferl.org/a/afghanistan-pakistan-soccer-unity-celebration/25082095.html |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220905151801/https://www.rferl.org/a/afghanistan-pakistan-soccer-unity-celebration/25082095.html |archive-date=5 September 2022 |accessdate=4 September 2022 |work=Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty}}</ref> Due to the [[Afghanistan–Pakistan relations|historical, cultural and political relationship]] between the two countries, contests between the teams generate much enthusiasm amongst football fans on account of their mutual relations and have been referred to as a "[[List of association football rivalries|rivalry]]".<ref name="RFERL">{{cite news |last=Bezhan |first=Frud |date=21 August 2013 |title=Historic Soccer Match Brings Unity To Afghanistan |url=https://www.rferl.org/a/afghanistan-pakistan-soccer-unity-celebration/25082095.html |accessdate=4 September 2022 |work=Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=20 August 2013 |title=Afghanistan beat Pakistan 3-0 in Kabul friendly |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/world-asia-23766790 |accessdate=12 September 2022 |work=BBC News}}</ref><ref>{{cite news |date=20 August 2013 |title=Confident Pakistan ready for historic Afghanistan clash |url=https://www.dawn.com/news/1037101 |accessdate=12 September 2022 |work=Dawn}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=20 August 2013 |title=Kabul fever ahead of Afghan-Pakistan clash |url=https://sg.news.yahoo.com/kabul-fever-ahead-afghan-pakistan-clash-222845717.html?guccounter=1&guce_referrer=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ29vZ2xlLmNvbS8&guce_referrer_sig=AQAAAHj2RL_R0iMemS51Fug8w0jwz4cEERx6ZhgmfHqFIiXSsbO5NfktmoiJEPclgiN3km6mtNxXxefcwXnLCijmfi4cMHW03ZjnSGBWkp-F_o4QjNrYh2CuxXh2q9nOvK_ye2QMd9KAcU1MiwgfmJntvCUYG8-KUQUEhhk0CR3MEFEo |access-date=14 August 2024 |website=Yahoo News |language=en-SG}}</ref>

== Results and fixtures ==
{{main|Afghanistan national football team results}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

! Role
! Name
|-
| Head coach
| {{flagicon|BRA}} [[José Antonio Nogueira]]<ref>{{cite web|access-date=2026-03-20 |date=2026-03-20 |language=en-EN |title=Nogueira Takes Charge of Afghanistan National Team
|website=ASJF News|url=https://asjfnews.com/nogueira-takes-charge-of-afghanistan-national-team/}}</ref>
|-
| Assistant coach
| {{flagicon|AFG|football}} Shabir Isoufi
|-
| Goalkeeping coach
| {{flagicon|AFG|football}} [[Hamidullah Yousafzai|Hamidullah Yousufzai]]
|-
| Equipment manager
| {{flagicon|AFG|football}} Mahdi Shirzad
|-
| Team manager
| {{flagicon|AFG|football}} Samir Homauni
|-
| Media manager
| {{flagicon|AFG|football}} Mustafa Hashimi

=== Managerial history ===
:{{Updated|30 August 2025}}
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.

{| class="wikitable"
! Name
! Period
! {{abbr|P|Played}}
! {{abbr|W|Won}}
! {{abbr|D|Drawn}}
! {{abbr|L|Lost}}
! {{abbr|Win %|Win percentage}}
! {{abbr|Ref.|Reference}}
|-
| {{flagicon|Afghanistan|1930}} Sardar Mohammad Farooq Khan Seraj
| 1949
{{WDL||||}}
| <ref>{{cite web | url=https://af.shafaqna.com/FA/60896 | title=برگی از تاریخ: سفر تیم ملی فوتبال افغانستان به تهران در سال 1328ش &#124; خبرگزاری شیعیان افغانستان &#124; Afghanistan - Shia News Agency | access-date=14 April 2023 | archive-date=14 April 2023 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230414065843/https://af.shafaqna.com/FA/60896 | url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.farsnews.ir/af/news/13940517000220/%D9%81%D9%88%D8%AA%D8%A8%D8%A7%D9%84%DB%8C%D8%B3%D8%AA%E2%80%8C%D9%87%D8%A7%DB%8C-%D8%A7%D9%81%D8%BA%D8%A7%D9%86%D8%B3%D8%AA%D8%A7%D9%86%DB%8C-%D8%AA%D9%87%D8%B1%D8%A7%D9%86-%D8%B1%D8%A7-%D8%AA%D8%B1%DA%A9-%DA%A9%D8%B1%D8%AF%D9%86%D8%AF|title=Home Page &#124; خبرگزاری فارس|website=www.farsnews.ir|access-date=14 April 2023|archive-date=14 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230414065841/https://www.farsnews.ir/af/news/13940517000220/%D9%81%D9%88%D8%AA%D8%A8%D8%A7%D9%84%DB%8C%D8%B3%D8%AA%E2%80%8C%D9%87%D8%A7%DB%8C-%D8%A7%D9%81%D8%BA%D8%A7%D9%86%D8%B3%D8%AA%D8%A7%D9%86%DB%8C-%D8%AA%D9%87%D8%B1%D8%A7%D9%86-%D8%B1%D8%A7-%D8%AA%D8%B1%DA%A9-%DA%A9%D8%B1%D8%AF%D9%86%D8%AF|url-status=live}}</ref>

| 1950–1974

== Players ==
=== Current squad ===
The following players were named in the squad for the [[2026 Diamond Jubilee International Football Tournament]] between 1–10 June 2026.

*''Caps and goals are correct as of 10 June 2026, after the match against [[Pakistan national football team|Pakistan]].''<ref>{{cite web |title=Afghanistan |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/1/Afghanistan.html |website=National Football Teams |access-date=30 August 2025}}</ref>

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=Faisal Hamidi|age={{birth date and age|1997|1|29|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[FC Sorkh Poshan|Sorkh Poshan Herat]]|clubnat=AFG}}

=== Recent call-ups ===
The following players have been called up for the team within the last 12 months and are still available for selection.
<!--Sorted by position, date of birth, caps, goals, and most recent call-up.-->

{{nat fs r player|no=10|pos=FW|name=[[Taufee Skandari]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|4|2|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=0|club=[[Abu Muslim FC|Abu Muslim]]|clubnat=AFG|latest=[[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=18|pos=FW|name=[[Hossein Zamani]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|19|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=1|club=[[FK Zvijezda 09|Zvijezda 09]]|clubnat=BIH|latest=[[2025 CAFA Nations Cup]]}}

{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Withdrew due to injury
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad
*<sup>RET</sup> = Retired from the national team
*<sup>SUS</sup> = Serving suspension
*<sup>WD</sup> = Withdrew due to non-injury related reasons
{{nat fs end}}

== Individual records ==
{{Updated|10 June 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Afghanistan |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/1/Afghanistan.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref> 
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Afghanistan.''

| {{flagicon|China}} [[Football at the 1990 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1990]]
|-

|-
| {{flagicon|Thailand}} [[Football at the 1998 Asian Games – Men's tournament|1998]]
|-
| [[Football at the 2002 Asian Games – Men's tournament|2002]] to present

|}
* {{small|Afghanistan were members of the [[South Asian Football Federation]] between 2005 and 2015. As a result, they did not enter the competition between 1993 and 1999 and have not entered it since 2015.}}

== Honours ==
=== Regional ===
* '''[[SAFF Championship]]'''
** [[File:Med 1.png]] '''Champions (1)''': [[2013 SAFF Championship|2013]]
** [[File:Med 2.png]] Runners-up (2): [[2011 SAFF Championship|2011]], [[2015 SAFF Championship|2015]]
* '''[[South Asian Games]]'''
** [[File:Med 2.png]] Silver Medal (1): [[Football at the 2010 South Asian Games|2010]]
